revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Bureau of Shrines and Temples]][[ja:社寺局]]


{{Short description|Japanese government organization|qq=}}
{{more citations needed|date=July 2022|qq=}}
{{Infobox organization
| formation = {{ill|1877|en|1877|qid=Q7773|lt=1877|1=1877}}
| predecessor = [[Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]]
| successor = [[Bureau of Shrines]], [[:en:Bureau of Religion|Bureau of Religion]]
| dissolved = April {{ill|1900|en|1900|qid=Q2034|lt=1900|1=1900}}
}}
The {{Nihongo|'''Bureau of Shrines and Temples.'''|社寺局|Shaji kyoku|qq=}}<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Glossary of Shinto Names and Terms: S |url=https://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/glossary/def_S.html#shaji_kyoku |access-date=2023-03-10 |website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp|qq=}}</ref> was a bureau of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji|1=Meiji era}} {{ill|Government|en|Government|qid=Q7188|lt=Government|1=Government}}.<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />
It was established in 1877 to administer matters related to religion, including {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=shrines|1=Shinto shrine}} and {{ill|Buddhist temples in Japan|en|Buddhist temples in Japan|qid=Q3141900|lt=temples|1=Buddhist temples in Japan}}, and [[Sect Shinto]] such as [[Tenrikyo]] and [[Kurozumikyō]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" /> It was a bureau of the {{ill|Home Ministry|en|Home Ministry|qid=Q1142265|lt=Home Ministry|1=Home Ministry}}.<ref name="秦郁彦編-1868a">{{ill|Ikuhiko Hata|en|Ikuhiko Hata|qid=Q5996291|lt=秦郁彦|1=Ikuhiko Hata}}編『日本官僚制総合事典：1868 - 2000』{{ill|University of Tokyo Press|en|University of Tokyo Press|qid=Q842460|lt=東京大学出版会|1=University of Tokyo Press}}、2001st year 。</ref><ref name="wwwjacargojp-2022a">{{Cite web |title=社寺局｜アジ歴グロッサリー |url=https://www.jacar.go.jp/glossary/term3/0010-0030-0040-0010-0200.html |access-date=2022-06-19 |website=www.jacar.go.jp|qq=}}</ref><ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />
In April {{ill|1900|en|1900|qid=Q2034|lt=1900|1=1900}} (33rd year of Meiji), the bureau was divided into two bureaus, the [[:en:Shinto shrine bureau|Bureau of Shrines]] and the [[:en:Bureau of Religion|Bureau of Religion]]. Temples, Christianity and new religions were transferred to the [[:en:Bureau of Religion|Bureau of Religion]].<ref name="秦郁彦編-1868a" /><ref name="wwwjacargojp-2022a"/><ref name="mullins-2022a">{{Cite journal |last=Mullins |first=Mark R. |last2=Thomas |first2=Jolyon Baraka |last3=McMullen |first3=Matthew D. |date=2022 |title=Religion, Politics, and the Law in Postwar Japan |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/48696755 |journal=Japanese Journal of Religious Studies |volume=49 |issue=1 |pages=118 |issn=0304-1042|qq=}}</ref> This was an official acknowledgement of [[Secular Shrine Theory]] or the idea that [[Shrine Shinto]] was not a religion and as a result under state control, hence the separate [[Bureau of Shrines]] under the {{ill|Home Ministry|en|Home Ministry|qid=Q1142265|lt=Home Ministry|1=Home Ministry}}.<ref name="mullins-2022a" />
== See also ==
* [[Department of Divinities]]
* [[Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]]
* {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|qid=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto|1=State Shinto}}
* [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]
